clean_body: The Food Crisis of 2023 We live in uncertain times. There are currently 38 million Americans, including 12 million children, who are struggling with food insecurity. As inflation and supply chain disruptions continue to push food costs higher and higher, families are feeling the weight of financial distress as they struggle to make ends meet. John Boyd Jr., a fourth-generation farmer, warns that food shortages are coming. "For so long, we've enjoyed lots of food in this country, so we've never ever faced a food shortage. The worst is yet to come." Leading industry sources are worried about food shortages. Experts say more grocery hoarding may come as disruptions push America's food supply "near its breaking point". As a result of this crisis, survival food is more important than ever. If you don't take action or if you stockpile the wrong foods, you could be setting your family up to be hungry in a time of crisis. It sounds harsh, but the truth is too many people with good intentions are making critical mistakes with their survival food. Mistakes like... Getting MREs with a 5-year shelf life - depending on where you purchase them from they could be near expired... Getting gross survival foods that are tough to stomach and so high in salt, MSG and preservatives you could clog your arteries and get yourself sick... Or simply getting the wrong foods and leaving a critical hole in your meal plan, which means you risk becoming malnourished... Well, no need to worry anymore... Obviously, waiting on the government for a handout in a disaster just isn't an option. And we were completely turned off by the crazy high cost of survival food sold by some companies. name: Gabe Vasquez
subject: Honored to be your U.S. Representative of NM-02
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY GABE VASQUEZ FOR CONGRESS
body:
| [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57071143/386783219/1850399781?refcode=fl_email_na_20230108sworn-in_gv_full_xx_hdr&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzAxMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmZTYzMDczNS05OThmLWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ldJ0ly6Zr8ibw66znUqQnp9YG4qyGudf9-uSzuVVvPU=)
---
Friend, **Yesterday at 1:40 am, it was the honor of a lifetime to (finally) be sworn in as your new U.S. Representative of New Mexico’s 2nd Congressional District.** Of course, it didn’t go as any of us had planned — after five long days and voting fifteen times to elect a Speaker of the House, the GOP circus finally ended and we were sworn into office in the early hours on a Saturday. Now, I’m ready to get to work. My own story has taught me to believe that this is the only place in the world where a son of immigrants from Mexico with barely more than the shirt on their backs could one day be elected to serve in Congress. As a first-generation American born and raised in the borderlands, I was given the opportunity to make a difference in my community as a staff member for U.S. Senator Martin Heinrich, a conservation advocate, and a Las Cruces City Councilor. **I vow to serve your best interests in Washington and work to ensure every New Mexican has an opportunity to succeed and live dignified, meaningful lives.** The hard work we put into winning this race paid off. Now, I’m going to work for you to lower costs and create new jobs, make prescription drugs affordable, fix our broken immigration system, let women make their own healthcare decisions, and protect our public lands, air, and water for generations to come. You have put your trust in me and, rest assured, I’m more than ready to fight for you. As this week has proved, it’s going to be challenging to get things done under a Republican majority, but I’m committed to overcoming the far-right chaos and delivering for you, the people. Thank you. _Vamos!_ — Gabe | [Donate](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57071144/386783220/1850399781?refcode=fl_email_na_20230108sworn-in_gv_full_xx_btn&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzAxMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmZTYzMDczNS05OThmLWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ldJ0ly6Zr8ibw66znUqQnp9YG4qyGudf9-uSzuVVvPU=)
---

| Gabe Vasquez is a first-generation American, former City Councilor, and conservation champion who flipped New Mexico’s 2nd Congressional District from red to blue in 2022 with the help of this people-powered movement. Now, he's taking his experience in office and passion for economic opportunities, the environment, and immigrant and civil rights to Congress to fight for the people of New Mexico.

clean_body: | = Friend, Yesterday at 1:40 am, it was the honor of a lifetime to (finally) be sworn in as your new U.S. Representative of New Mexico’s 2nd Congressional District. Of course, it didn’t go as any of us had planned — after five long days and voting fifteen times to elect a Speaker of the House, the GOP circus finally ended and we were sworn into office in the early hours on a Saturday. Now, I’m ready to get to work. My own story has taught me to believe that this is the only place in the world where a son of immigrants from Mexico with barely more than the shirt on their backs could one day be elected to serve in Congress. As a first-generation American born and raised in the borderlands, I was given the opportunity to make a difference in my community as a staff member for U.S. Senator Martin Heinrich, a conservation advocate, and a Las Cruces City Councilor. I vow to serve your best interests in Washington and work to ensure every New Mexican has an opportunity to succeed and live dignified, meaningful lives. The hard work we put into winning this race paid off. Now, I’m going to work for you to lower costs and create new jobs, make prescription drugs affordable, fix our broken immigration system, let women make their own healthcare decisions, and protect our public lands, air, and water for generations to come. You have put your trust in me and, rest assured, I’m more than ready to fight for you. As this week has proved, it’s going to be challenging to get things done under a Republican majority, but I’m committed to overcoming the far-right chaos and delivering for you, the people. Thank you. Vamos! — Gabe | = | Gabe Vasquez is a first-generation American, former City Councilor, and conservation champion who flipped New Mexico’s 2nd Congressional District from red to blue in 2022 with the help of this people-powered movement. Now, he's taking his experience in office and passion for economic opportunities, the environment, and immigrant and civil rights to Congress to fight for the people of New Mexico. | = | PAID FOR BY GABE VASQUEZ FOR CONGRESS Gabe Vasquez for Congress Drawer L Mesilla, NM 88046 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please .

name: Gabby Giffords
subject: Today is the 10 year anniversary of Giffords
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
I hope you’ll allow me to share some reflections with you on this special day for our organization.
#

Friends, today is January 8th, 2023: The ten year anniversary of Giffords. I hope you’ll allow me to share some reflections with you on this special day for our organization.
While a lot of people might think it was my own shooting that inspired me to commit my life to ending gun violence, that wasn’t the case. The shooting at Sandy Hook Elementary School in Newtown, Connecticut was the reason I decided to devote myself to this fight.
I’ll never forget the day of the Sandy Hook shooting. Twenty children and six teachers were killed. These were innocent people whose lives were stolen by the actions of a gunman driven by hatred. Like many Americans, I watched in disbelief as the events of that day unfolded.
Even though I was in the midst of my own recovery, I knew I couldn’t just sit back and do nothing after this tragedy. At the time, my husband Mark was in Asia. I called him, and I remember the one word that came to my mind to express what I was feeling.
_“Enough.”_
Mark was right there with me in his feelings of shock and horror. So three weeks after this tragedy, we went to Newtown and met personally with families who lost loved ones, and other parents who had children in the school that day.
That was one of the hardest days of my life. I remember the thoughts echoing in my head after that day: _Children are dying. We must do something._
With that, the idea for our organization was born. After weeks of gathering a leadership team, planning, and fundraising, we launched Americans for Responsible Solutions on January 8th, 2013, exactly two years after a gunman tried to take my life in Tucson and killed six of my constituents.
My goal for this organization—what we know today as Giffords—was to bring together concerned Americans from all backgrounds who were tired of the gun violence plaguing our country and wanted to take action.
Although we’ve made progress, we’ve also experienced some devastating lows. I’ll never forget the day in 2013 when the Senate failed to pass bipartisan background checks legislation. Today, we’re still fighting for universal background checks—a commensense solution that would save lives.
We’re still in the midst of a gun violence epidemic in our country. Sandy Hook was unfortunately not the last school shooting our nation faced. We had Parkland. We had Uvalde. In 2017, we had the deadliest mass shooting in modern American history at the Route 91 Harvest Music Festival in Las Vegas. And we can’t ignore the daily violence in homes and communities nationwide that often doesn’t make the news.
So yes, we have a long way to go to secure a future where nobody feels unsafe to attend school, shop at the supermarket, go to the movie theater, or simply walk down the street. I will not rest until that future becomes a reality. You can count on that.
I know sometimes the lows may make it seem like progress is impossible in the fight to end gun violence. This is tireless work, but it is worth it. I promise you. The hardest fights always are.
Change is possible, and it’s happening. We’ve changed hearts, changed minds, and changed laws. We’ve had some incredible triumphs since our founding ten years ago. 525 gun safety laws have been passed in states across the country in the last decade. In 2022, the most significant federal gun safety law in nearly three decades was signed into law. But the work continues.
I know this has been a long message, and I want to thank you for taking the time to read. Now is the time where I have to make an important ask of you.
[**In honor of ten years of Giffords, will you please chip in $10? If you’ve been waiting to make your first online contribution to Giffords, there truly is no better day than this monumental day.**](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-jan23.1ic_u0&refcode=em230108-1-15&t=1&akid=3955%2E3935704%2EZh_7pt)
**[CONTRIBUTE NOW](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-jan23.1ic_u0&refcode=em230108-1-15&t=2&akid=3955%2E3935704%2EZh_7pt)**
Whether you’ve been with us from the start, or have just joined our movement recently, I want to thank you so so much. It takes many voices to make a difference, and we can only do our work to achieve a future without gun violence through the support of people like you.
With courage,
Gabby Giffords
**[CONTRIBUTE NOW](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-jan23.1ic_u0&refcode=em230108-1-15&t=3&akid=3955%2E3935704%2EZh_7pt)**

Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. www.Giffords.org
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com . We try to send only the most important information and opportunities to participate via email, but click [here](https://go.giffords.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=3955%2E3935704%2EZh_7pt) if you'd like to unsubscribe. Giffords PAC was started to support elected officials who step up to fight the gun violence epidemic. Thank you for standing with Gabby and the Giffords team.

name: Katie Porter
subject: Finally sworn in
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Congress
body:
Kevin McCarthy was able to win the support of his caucus by promising to empower extremists and further tip the scale towards special interests.
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/12?t=1&akid=15626%2E3422442%2E8NbmTW)
Derek,
In the very early hours of Saturday morning, I was finally sworn in to Congress, after Republicans officially elected Kevin McCarthy as Speaker—on the fifteenth try.
Kevin McCarthy was able to win the support of his caucus by promising to empower extremists and further tip the scale towards special interests. Americans deserve better than Kevin McCarthy’s loyalty to some of the most hateful members of his party.
Now, I’m glad to be back home in California and ready to continue serving Orange County families.
Katie
_If you’re curious about why the House was at a standstill all week, check out this email I sent on Thursday explaining the process of electing a Speaker._
* * *
Hey Derek, I emailed you on Tuesday saying I would be sworn in...well, turns out, the Republicans’ inability to elect a Speaker means that didn’t happen. This is highly unusual, so let me explain what’s going on:
**To be elected Speaker of the House, a Member needs a majority of the votes**(that magic number is 218). Typically, the party in power figures out who they’ll be electing in advance, and the Speaker vote is largely ceremonial. But this year, twenty Republicans have decided to _oppose_ the presumed Speaker, Kevin McCarthy. Without their votes, McCarthy doesn’t have the majority he needs to be made Speaker.
We don’t know what is happening behind closed doors in the Republican caucus, but Kevin McCarthy’s only move at this point is to continue bringing this vote to the floor. And each time (we’ve voted on this six times already!) he fails to win the majority.
**So who is going to be Speaker of the House? The very real answer is I’m not sure!**
Here’s what I do know: **The House can’t do *anything* until we have a Speaker.** We can’t swear in Members, we can’t operate committees, we can’t vote on bills. That’s just how Congress operates. We need a Speaker of the House in order to proceed.
For the foreseeable future, I will be here in D.C., waiting for Republicans to cut the BS and elect a Speaker. It’s been 100 years since a Speaker failed to be elected after a first vote, so we’re all guessing what the next move will be.
This certainly does feel like an episode of a political TV show—I’ll be sure to keep you updated.
Katie Porter
**[Donate ›››](https://act.katieporter.com/go/14212?refcode=EMAF08_SPE_20230108_V1_F&t=2&akid=15626%2E3422442%2E8NbmTW)**

Paid for by Katie Porter for Congress
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you would like to receive fewer emails, please [**click here**](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/receive-fewer-kp-emails/). To contribute via check, please address to: Katie Porter for Congress, PO Box 5176, Irvine, CA 92616

**Attorney General Jason Miyares and Georgia AG Chris Carr led a group of 14 state attorneys general in sending a letter to U.S. AG Garland inquiring what the U.S. Department of Justice “has done or is currently doing” to address the potent threat that third-party litigation funding (TPLF) poses to economic and national security.** **_TPLF is a lucrative practice, valued between $2.3 and $5 billion, where an outside third party supplies resources to fund civil disputes in exchange for a share of the recovery if successful. Through TPLF, foreign adversaries can invest in American litigation with the intention of harming States and threatening the wellbeing of the United States’ economic and national security._** _“The newest threat to our American experiment lies in third-party litigation funding by hostile foreign entities. We cannot let Russia or China fund attacks on our justice system that weaken our country. The federal government has been silent on this issue for far too long.”_ **– Attorney General Miyares**

**Attorney General Miyares’ Major Crimes and Emerging Threats Unit successfully prosecuted an individual for firearm and fentanyl distribution offenses.****** _“My Major Crimes and Emerging Threats Unit works tirelessly to protect our communities and make our Commonwealth safer. I’m proud of the work they’ve done here to help get fentanyl off our streets and protecting the lives they’ve likely saved by doing so.”_ **– Attorney General Miyares**

In light of an increase in anti-Semitic hate across the country, especially within institutions of higher education, Attorney General Miyares wrote a letter to state university and college leaders to warn about the prevalence of antisemitism on university grounds throughout America. **_According to the Anti-Defamation League, over 350 anti-Semitic incidents took place on university and college campuses during the 2021-22 academic year._** **Attorney General Miyares made it clear that religious discrimination of any kind will not be tolerated in the Commonwealth of Virginia, requesting that institutions maintain awareness of this alarming trend and do what they can to condemn anti-Semitic hate.**

**TikTok Ban on State Devices & Networks**
---

_“Not only does TikTok pose a threat to national security and consumer privacy, but studies have shown that it negatively impacts the mental health of our youth. In March, I joined a bipartisan coalition of 43 other attorneys general to investigate TikTok’s physical and mental impact on children. As this investigation continues, I am glad that Governor Youngkin is addressing the serious security risks TikTok poses for the Commonwealth.”_ **– Attorney General Miyares**

_“One pill can take a life. One pill can rob you of your dreams. One pill can deny your family of seeing you again, of ever having a normal holiday because you had your life taken too soon. Part of our job is to remind people that mistakes will not define your life, that your past shouldn’t define your future, and that the biggest sign of strength if you’re dealing with addiction is just admitting that you need help.”_ **– Attorney General Jason Miyares** **Take the time to have the tough conversations with your loved ones regarding the fentanyl and counterfeit drug epidemic sweeping the nation.** **You could save a life.**

clean_body: | | | Happy New Year! | | This week, Attorney General Miyares announced two Civil Rights investigations into Thomas Jefferson High School for Science and Technology (“TJHSST”) regarding the school’s admission policy and withholding of merit awards. The Office of Civil Rights will investigate any potential violations of the law and vindicate the civil rights of these students and their families. Attorney General Miyares joined discuss the announcement of the investigation. Attorney General Miyares wishes all a Happy New Year filled with joy and laughter! | | Attorney General Jason Miyares and Georgia AG Chris Carr led a group of 14 state attorneys general in sending a letter to U.S. AG Garland inquiring what the U.S. Department of Justice “has done or is currently doing” to address the potent threat that third-party litigation funding (TPLF) poses to economic and national security. TPLF is a lucrative practice, valued between $2.3 and $5 billion, where an outside third party supplies resources to fund civil disputes in exchange for a share of the recovery if successful. Through TPLF, foreign adversaries can invest in American litigation with the intention of harming States and threatening the wellbeing of the United States’ economic and national security. “The newest threat to our American experiment lies in third-party litigation funding by hostile foreign entities. We cannot let Russia or China fund attacks on our justice system that weaken our country. The federal government has been silent on this issue for far too long.” – Attorney General Miyares Attorney General Miyares’ Major Crimes and Emerging Threats Unit successfully prosecuted an individual for firearm and fentanyl distribution offenses.** “My Major Crimes and Emerging Threats Unit works tirelessly to protect our communities and make our Commonwealth safer. I’m proud of the work they’ve done here to help get fentanyl off our streets and protecting the lives they’ve likely saved by doing so.” – Attorney General Miyares In light of an increase in anti-Semitic hate across the country, especially within institutions of higher education, Attorney General Miyares wrote a letter to state university and college leaders to warn about the prevalence of antisemitism on university grounds throughout America. According to the Anti-Defamation League, over 350 anti-Semitic incidents took place on university and college campuses during the 2021-22 academic year. Attorney General Miyares made it clear that religious discrimination of any kind will not be tolerated in the Commonwealth of Virginia, requesting that institutions maintain awareness of this alarming trend and do what they can to condemn anti-Semitic hate. "Virginia Attorney General Sends Letter Addressing National Rise in Antisemitism to VA School Leaders" TikTok Ban on State Devices & Networks “Not only does TikTok pose a threat to national security and consumer privacy, but studies have shown that it negatively impacts the mental health of our youth. In March, I joined a bipartisan coalition of 43 other attorneys general to investigate TikTok’s physical and mental impact on children. As this investigation continues, I am glad that Governor Youngkin is addressing the serious security risks TikTok poses for the Commonwealth.” – Attorney General Miyares “One pill can take a life. One pill can rob you of your dreams. One pill can deny your family of seeing you again, of ever having a normal holiday because you had your life taken too soon. Part of our job is to remind people that mistakes will not define your life, that your past shouldn’t define your future, and that the biggest sign of strength if you’re dealing with addiction is just admitting that you need help.” – Attorney General Jason Miyares Take the time to have the tough conversations with your loved ones regarding the fentanyl and counterfeit drug epidemic sweeping the nation. You could save a life. ---
Katie Hobbs thought she was such a great cheater that there was no chance she'd ever get caught.

She was wrong. In a short, two-day trial, my team uncovered a treasure trove of evidence proving that the November election was blatantly influenced and disenfranchised Republican voters to help Katie Hobbs win.

Every day that we keep fighting the morally corrupt and unAmerican shadow cabal pulling the strings in Arizona are shaking in their boots.

| | [ ](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953w5gOujJU4G2FQlGZnk4uqAhEqs6AkSYBRHgC_6K6_YCZyBtnuvEUtm5Du6Eg4MeEmi4nemSrTPG1ikxn9owWsvt_OO6IfIQD7fITVJq6viCLS4lNXmBxNBPMMlcaMLSoreLV0PBch_2d220oQV8ruyu50evGBuVk_r4DC3jHA7AaGsnjXdFwZrXkf2o9kd6IDFdRuvFK1BgxTxeJQwxvnlpF1B_omBRR7WL_U4kWweFbt5ZTwGkXoit6sg5y8abeAK8NyZd_kw1t99JhKAkRIdlEJxr84pgWwOoUTv1BPjqhizmLRludhfsOlUPN_zshnX6yBT3JwXGrDBqn3cPA8MAgqJRyf6FO1_34BoyGiDqA/3so/s66JpnyhQg-3erAua6-T8w/h0/cNsg94CNPOKUJhkBfuaXpQLpVLmR-jiSJOBdIA2l3oo)
---
It's (finally) official, Peter: As of late Friday night, I'm now Congressman Eric Sorensen – representing Illinois 17th District.
---
| [ ](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953w5gOujJU4G2FQlGZnk4uqAhEqs6AkSYBRHgC_6K6_YCZyBtnuvEUtm5Du6Eg4MeEmi4nemSrTPG1ikxn9owWsvt_OO6IfIQD7fITVJq6viCOEkJOYwnm3mRIAUT46csRzsuR8KPQne9UlRIT6dTz-26EgelKCgsUB3Q4pXReTMVKiXzZB2th6NCejF-ey_pPytFL7AfWWtTpyFmo5zWxs3kPv_ulehYioQT8UQ3swmRJxOs4pEmQ1fDZImgTQ6MZxrV7X5kd-0VO2qk0hrPaRjMZrNMw6zsZNlOZgtvTQPASWSUcRFKF6JLxsb-IpjEHiYRO8zkOMfjZu1ry9vHbLwMc8NugNCzSZHq4VquIcaZw/3so/s66JpnyhQg-3erAua6-T8w/h1/82UXevLgxwyNqzOG-twzhkuILYae4tpzonLegPtCOOE)
---
I have to admit, this isn't exactly how I envisioned kicking off my first term in Congress. It took the Republicans a full five days and 15 attempts before they could get their act together and elect a new Speaker. They’re already proving incapable of leading in 2023, which means it's going to take all my effort and more to cut through the chaos and gridlock to make progress for IL-17. But I’m ready. A story like mine doesn't usually lead to Capitol Hill. For starters, I'm not a politician; I'm a meteorologist who spent two decades serving my Northwest and Central Illinois communities. I'm also the first LGBTQ person to represent Illinois in Congress. I'm proud to be breaking new ground, but from the start this race was only about electing a representative who truly has our interests at heart. It was about protecting our democracy and securing reproductive rights, and it was about making sure IL-17 communities can grow and thrive for generations to come. **Now that I'm in Congress, my name may be on the door, but this office belongs to the people I serve, and I fully intend to treat it that way.** I'm not wasting time, Peter. We have an incredible opportunity to make progress on issues that affect countless lives: health care, the economy, protecting the environment, and so much more. I look forward to continuing to engage with my supporters. I'm asking you to hold me accountable and keep pushing me and my colleagues to do more, because we are here to serve you, and your input is critical to our work. Thanks for your support. Now, let’s get to work. Eric
---

body:
Congratulations, Congresswoman Becca Balint! | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57071561/386785323/966991095?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOTg2YWE0ZC1hMjhmLWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2HmV9pktYM-5AHLtrU9Sj6FVjAiABVqpetGFs5P4vOw=&emci=25f90039-818b-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&emdi=a986aa4d-a28f-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&ceid=43402272) | It’s official, Peter: **Becca has been sworn in as Vermont’s next member of Congress!** [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57071562/386785324/2020475125?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOTg2YWE0ZC1hMjhmLWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2HmV9pktYM-5AHLtrU9Sj6FVjAiABVqpetGFs5P4vOw=&emci=25f90039-818b-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&emdi=a986aa4d-a28f-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&ceid=43402272) Becca will represent Vermonters in Washington with the same courage and kindness that has defined her work and leadership here at home. We know that she’s ready to fight for every single one of us on the issues that matter most – from housing to mental health care, to fair wages and abortion rights. **This moment calls for celebration – and a HUGE congratulations to Vermont’s newest (and FIRST) Congresswoman![Sign on now to send Becca a personal note of congratulations on her swearing-in!](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57071563/386785325/2020475125?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOTg2YWE0ZC1hMjhmLWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2HmV9pktYM-5AHLtrU9Sj6FVjAiABVqpetGFs5P4vOw=&emci=25f90039-818b-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&emdi=a986aa4d-a28f-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&ceid=43402272)** [CONGRATS CONGRESSWOMAN!](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57071564/386785326/2020475125?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOTg2YWE0ZC1hMjhmLWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2HmV9pktYM-5AHLtrU9Sj6FVjAiABVqpetGFs5P4vOw=&emci=25f90039-818b-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&emdi=a986aa4d-a28f-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&ceid=43402272) Since Day One, Becca has been committed to using her platform to uplift the voices and priorities of real Vermonters: The teachers, baristas, firefighters, laborers, students, farm workers, small business owners, caretakers, and social workers who power Vermont’s economy and our way of life. Every vote Becca takes, coalition she builds, and bill she authors will be in the spirit of doing the most good for _all of us_ – and we’re so proud to have her representing our communities in Washington. **With Becca now officially sworn in as our newest member of Congress,[take a moment to congratulate her and send her a personal note as she kicks off her work in the 118th Congress!](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57071565/386785327/2020475125?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOTg2YWE0ZC1hMjhmLWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2HmV9pktYM-5AHLtrU9Sj6FVjAiABVqpetGFs5P4vOw=&emci=25f90039-818b-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&emdi=a986aa4d-a28f-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&ceid=43402272)** Thank you for being a part of Becca’s big day! – Team Becca
---

* * *
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57071566/386785328/537668883?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOTg2YWE0ZC1hMjhmLWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2HmV9pktYM-5AHLtrU9Sj6FVjAiABVqpetGFs5P4vOw=&emci=25f90039-818b-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&emdi=a986aa4d-a28f-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&ceid=43402272) [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57071567/386785329/-1209379614?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOTg2YWE0ZC1hMjhmLWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2HmV9pktYM-5AHLtrU9Sj6FVjAiABVqpetGFs5P4vOw=&emci=25f90039-818b-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&emdi=a986aa4d-a28f-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&ceid=43402272) [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57071568/386785330/411952208?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOTg2YWE0ZC1hMjhmLWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2HmV9pktYM-5AHLtrU9Sj6FVjAiABVqpetGFs5P4vOw=&emci=25f90039-818b-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&emdi=a986aa4d-a28f-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&ceid=43402272)
**Becca believes the best thoughts are not always from the loudest voices in the room.** As President Pro Tem of the Vermont State Senate, she's led the charge to invest in affordable housing, fight climate change, enshrine reproductive freedom into our state's constitution, and so much more. Now, she's making history as the first woman and openly LGBTQ person to ever serve Vermont in Congress. clean_body: Congratulations, Congresswoman Becca Balint! | | It’s official, Peter: Becca has been sworn in as Vermont’s next member of Congress! Becca will represent Vermonters in Washington with the same courage and kindness that has defined her work and leadership here at home. We know that she’s ready to fight for every single one of us on the issues that matter most – from housing to mental health care, to fair wages and abortion rights. This moment calls for celebration – and a HUGE congratulations to Vermont’s newest (and FIRST) Congresswoman Since Day One, Becca has been committed to using her platform to uplift the voices and priorities of real Vermonters: The teachers, baristas, firefighters, laborers, students, farm workers, small business owners, caretakers, and social workers who power Vermont’s economy and our way of life. Every vote Becca takes, coalition she builds, and bill she authors will be in the spirit of doing the most good for all of us – and we’re so proud to have her representing our communities in Washington. With Becca now officially sworn in as our newest member of Congress, Thank you for being a part of Becca’s big day! – Team Becca Becca believes the best thoughts are not always from the loudest voices in the room. As President Pro Tem of the Vermont State Senate, she's led the charge to invest in affordable housing, fight climate change, enshrine reproductive freedom into our state's constitution, and so much more. Now, she's making history as the first woman and openly LGBTQ person to ever serve Vermont in Congress. name: Reclaiming American Freedom
subject: Victor Davis Hanson on DVD
body:
Only a fraction of the 16 million Americans who fought bravely in World War II remain with us. This sponsored message from Reclaiming American Freedom is on behalf of Hillsdale College [](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=541454e50d054c27a5b2d83934413ab1&_e=T_z6tlmyCBWEbD89FG6BoaUWBtyMP5cGj1ORAzRgCrFDBu8G_cjV9U4fpRJY7Jxx6VqftlnQHXVGkKbORrntJMPM1cqco7SHY0BmEGYBridhroKvL_VIonZv251OJRSlGwNbo6UyX0zvqL8Cz5lahCDE0IbJjuQmSqOZIlW2Rke-u_AmgYMaECUk9Ap9PYoTafzp5ISKvvpyVFWBVwnMlpd77c9N3Uo6hXeVgt9oBpsMHLKLAi6j3gJHoq_0-VuZiFD1vPsTJdFAVBEceUFHqxyHEd4Y3ChEwMuJDZNY-gHz1OewOVrkRZFTKqxAAkCvPsK0RcC4oZ6MFFwnKCAZk9ousi9Sga0qD3jLVK4vPIQGO22GxUTQhnahSAkI33Jk9S65aQ-eMds31vEW7WJtjw%3D%3D) Dear Fellow American,
Only a fraction of the 16 million Americans who fought bravely in World War II remain with us. This fact means that we’re losing more and more living memory of the greatest armed conflict in human history, which makes the study of World War II even more important. To this end, Hillsdale College has produced an online course entitled “The Second World Wars.” Taught by historian Victor Davis Hanson, this course explores how the conflict’s diverse theaters, belligerents, and ways of fighting came eventually to define a single war. And it examines the key investments, strategies, and leaders that led the Allied powers to victory over the Axis. [](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=541454e50d054c27a5b2d83934413ab1&_e=T_z6tlmyCBWEbD89FG6BoaUWBtyMP5cGj1ORAzRgCrFDBu8G_cjV9U4fpRJY7Jxx6VqftlnQHXVGkKbORrntJMPM1cqco7SHY0BmEGYBridhroKvL_VIonZv251OJRSlGwNbo6UyX0zvqL8Cz5lahCDE0IbJjuQmSqOZIlW2Rke-u_AmgYMaECUk9Ap9PYoTafzp5ISKvvpyVFWBVwnMlpd77c9N3Uo6hXeVgt9oBpsMHLKLAi6j3gJHoq_0-VuZsiGzgoMxbeOAUwzd1UavvMdYjAMAjayrtzsHF-lZ1DH208mlTs4DZJ_rJHEI7T36MHNFlWqqXDfjAz52cauvlIx1ej2r3vL2w2BWoyzC8i1QzmiMII7NlXhH70nrUknDVtDCoYRbY98Tp2deAOfx8A%3D%3D) If you’d like to secure a DVD set before they’re all gone, you can get one shipped directly to your home for any gift of $100 or more to Hillsdale College. Here’s a secure link to reserve your copy of “**The Second World Wars** ” on DVD: **[https://secured.hillsdale.edu/hillsdale/support-the-second-world-wars](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=541454e50d054c27a5b2d83934413ab1&_e=T_z6tlmyCBWEbD89FG6BoaUWBtyMP5cGj1ORAzRgCrFDBu8G_cjV9U4fpRJY7Jxx6VqftlnQHXVGkKbORrntJMPM1cqco7SHY0BmEGYBridhroKvL_VIonZv251OJRSlGwNbo6UyX0zvqL8Cz5lahCDE0IbJjuQmSqOZIlW2Rke-u_AmgYMaECUk9Ap9PYoTafzp5ISKvvpyVFWBVwnMlpd77c9N3Uo6hXeVgt9oBpsMHLKLAi6j3gJHoq_0-VuZYXzbuu9KWK5OVmZuAldCsVkIVoHCPwbq4wNc64q6_vpkrzzioUiN80V-DkcZhP_71mnx2gpZGmIWGMezivE01E4uIRfjzdvccWpJBSyJ4X2EbNvUzBF9PlrRgkoY7WKxsiONBibpwvcei5NtZkidGQ%3D%3D)** Thank you for preserving liberty for future generations of Americans through education with Hillsdale College! # [Responses to Populism Show Why Democrats Are Failing](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=95459187&utm_source=post-email-title&isFreemail=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6OTU0NTkxODcsImlhdCI6MTY3MzIwMzgxMSwiZXhwIjoxNjc1Nzk1ODExLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTAxODU5NyIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.7CJtQ2MvHKn9KXNi0HwAQ58Iw9c-tEwrzEPI9UnWvKI)
### An uprising within the GOP should shame "progressive" Democrats
| | [Shahid Buttar](https://substack.com/redirect/b3605318-fab7-4256-b762-b62c0b767734?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

This week, the Washington punditry critiqued a seeming exhibition of congressional dysfunction as the new GOP House majority struggled to reach consensus before eventually making Rep. Kevin McCarthy (R-CA) the new Speaker of the House. Dissent within the Republican ranks forced 15 rounds of voting before Trump's reported [intervention](https://substack.com/redirect/a166d0e0-e36c-42b0-8087-016a7c66d5a1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), coupled with a variety of [concessions](https://substack.com/redirect/84c62ca9-61a0-4be9-9600-bc36eea32a38?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to the populist flank, finally won McCarthy a majority after several days of voting.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e5ffb56f-6170-44be-8911-e0956e7abc5a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Many of McCarthy's concessions to the GOP right wing have been criticized by both Democrats and centrist Republicans. Some of those concessions, however--not to mention the tactics that secured them--offer potentially surprising lessons for populists in the Democratic Party who have yet to achieve the same influence as those in the GOP. While Republicans may be caught in the mounting crosswinds of populist outrage towards Washington, the corporate center continues to dominate the Democratic Party.
### **History might not repeat itself, but it certainly rhymes**
On the first anniversary of the January 6 insurrection, a pair of members of Congress engaged in [a physical altercation](https://substack.com/redirect/5b66c303-9457-4e05-bed1-88b1be279535?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on the House floor for the first time in decades. One, Mike Rogers (R-AL), was restrained by another, Richard Hudson (R-NC), while angrily approaching a third, Matt Gaetz (R-FL), seemingly in retaliation for Gaetz's refusal to support McCarthy in the 14th round of voting.
Lost on no one was the timing of the event: exactly two years--to the very date--of the violent insurrection that shook the world's confidence in Washington and led to dozens of arrests and convictions of participants across the United States.
Looming in the future are the likely repetitions of other recent examples of government dysfunction, particularly the [shutdowns](https://substack.com/redirect/438b58a9-55e6-4632-b02d-2d6878b48bab?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that shuttered government agencies across the country in 1995, 2013, 2018, and 2019. With the GOP's populist majority emboldened by the concessions they won from McCarthy (including a reduced threshold for considering a new Speaker, limits on government & Pentagon spending, and separate votes on each of the government appropriation bills that Democrats have historically lumped together in order to derail debate & deliberation), they are well poised to repeat the tactics that previously forced federal agencies to furlough hundreds of thousands of employees around the country who [went without pay](https://substack.com/redirect/00f27e75-41db-4d2a-8ea7-41cd4ffc743a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for months at a time.
### **Democracy is at risk, but Democrats mistake the source of the threat**
Many Democrats have [observed](https://substack.com/redirect/f7f50df4-c9ff-4bf4-9e12-c69e224f82fd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the GOP's weak commitment to democracy, while conveniently overlooking their own party's thoroughly documented complicity in authoritarianism.
The January 6 insurrection certainly offended democracy. Responding to congressional certification of a presidential vote by storming the Capitol is hardly a reflection of a peaceful transition of power.
As I've noted elsewhere, the January 6 insurrection showed how little right wing populists actually care about the "law & order" they claim to champion when confronting advocates for police reform or abolition, while also demonstrating the [uselessness](https://substack.com/redirect/6e3cfb72-8912-4e6a-929b-d140f30c17fe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--and corresponding [fraud](https://substack.com/redirect/9e83421b-6f6e-4f0e-8555-9c2c3921dc5b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--of Washington's various duplicative and fraudulent "security" budgets, as well as the community of law enforcement professionals who proved to be [unfortunately complicit](https://substack.com/redirect/8574d3f3-45c4-44d7-af32-b5db7589790a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the insurrection.
But the insurrection offended democracy no less so than the preceding decades of congressional consensus supporting runaway increases in military spending, recurring tax cuts for millionaires, bailouts for businesses, and subsidies for fossil fuel corporations at the same time that Congress has frozen the federal minimum wage for well over a decade. Each of these decisions reflect a broad bipartisan consensus among politicians supported by corporate interests, conspicuously ignoring the voices of the millions of people across America and around the world who are impacted by them.
The biggest threat to democracy in America is the [bipartisan consensus](https://substack.com/redirect/39c9a7a9-9a54-4d38-b2d7-1702f83e603b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) supporting impunity for the executive branch and its continuing violations of civil liberties and civil rights across the United States.
Whining about democracy while focusing on the theatrics of the GOP majority risks leaping from a frying pan into a fire, or perhaps ducking into a punch. The biggest threat to democracy in America is not the specter of confusion surrounding our elections. They have exhibited [consistent](https://substack.com/redirect/cccc42a8-944f-4a09-aa9c-014b8a71b595?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) irregularities since at least [2000](https://substack.com/redirect/71540b3a-63b7-4056-8111-38f6fa0b0212?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) while at best being reduced to corporate fundraising contests, all ultimately steered by the [omissions](https://substack.com/redirect/b738dc92-8228-4889-92cc-3f4cd75c09b6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [fabrications](https://substack.com/redirect/4901a133-edd8-48f7-badc-9044b81ca41c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and self-promoting [navel gazing](https://substack.com/redirect/0fcc9d58-7a14-4fb8-8100-61746780c8d0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of journalists beholden to the establishment.
Even if attacks on voting rights and procedural democracy were the greatest threat to our Republic, Democrats would remain complicit. They continue to fight to [block Green Party candidates](https://substack.com/redirect/84617911-cca7-4b6a-a2ca-4c559df61718?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from ballot access, and have long played an active role in the arms race that has reduced elections to corporate fundraising contests. Some Democrats, including the current President of the United States and the former Speaker of the House, have even openly advocated for--and even gone so far as to [unilaterally](https://substack.com/redirect/a9365286-2c1b-47fc-8534-bf861e28f809?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [impose](https://substack.com/redirect/3adec6b5-8d29-4621-a819-673f743cf6cb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--Republican policies after winning office as Democrats.
Any of these measures verge on election fraud. Together, they reveal the hypocrisy of Democrats who claim to care about democracy.
[Shahid Buttar (find me on Substack) @ShahidForChangeThe January 6 insurrection is, at this point, a sideshow. The threats to our democracy extend beyond a single event, or ideological network. *Fascism is bipartisan.* It has also been well-established for far longer than most white Americans realize. Shahid Buttar (find me on Substack) @ShahidForChangeEvery institutional abuse we have witnessed from the Trump administration--from DHS goon squads suppressing dissent to eugenics at concentration camps--has required Nancy Pelosi to write the checks. https://t.co/fBS5ooR3bc](https://substack.com/redirect/39c9a7a9-9a54-4d38-b2d7-1702f83e603b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)[2:45 PM ∙ Jun 10, 2022
* * *
40Likes18Retweets](https://substack.com/redirect/39c9a7a9-9a54-4d38-b2d7-1702f83e603b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The biggest threat to democracy in America remains the [bipartisan consensus](https://substack.com/redirect/39c9a7a9-9a54-4d38-b2d7-1702f83e603b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) supporting impunity for the executive branch and its continuing violations of civil liberties and civil rights across the United States. That theme has defined my work for well over a decade, and the emergence of support within Washington for long overdue scrutiny of the defense and intelligence establishment's corruption is welcome.
It is also deeply ironic, considering the source.
### **Can you hear me now?**
I've been publicly pleading for nearly 20 years for Congress to finally investigate the national security agencies that it caught in the 1970s after decades of assaulting peaceful Americans and their constitutional rights. I've [written](https://substack.com/redirect/4c6acc0a-c8cc-4401-a50e-573f03b58e4c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [dozens](https://substack.com/redirect/09556a00-1c9b-4318-a1ed-2df2079b7d18?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of [articles](https://substack.com/redirect/1b0e4407-fe37-4a31-8d7e-fc2330e43956?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [spoken](https://substack.com/redirect/f97bea25-58a2-49e7-9e71-5ddfdea74968?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) at [hundreds](https://substack.com/redirect/18cd0590-9822-4f73-9008-e89b91853df5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of [events](https://substack.com/redirect/908faf35-0f6c-4dce-aab1-cf8ea96820f5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) around the country, and [advocated](https://substack.com/redirect/a85e4abe-b7ba-4e11-a421-cf2fb2f3c502?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in more [settings](https://substack.com/redirect/99ef092f-a515-4f65-8cc9-6383c8493298?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) than I can [count](https://substack.com/redirect/d6279bdc-fecd-41bc-908f-9c4eea3f291c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for democratically elected policymakers to scrutinize--and impose [restrictions](https://substack.com/redirect/a0c36eb3-439d-47cf-aaa1-8b2fb3182e14?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) limiting--executive branch agencies that have run amok for three generations.
My arrest in the U.S. Senate in 2015 was prompted by my posing a question that no policymaker has ever demonstrated the independence to ask: Why do executive branch officials who get caught lying to Congress enjoy prestige and public pensions, while powerless Black Americans are routinely killed without charge, trial, or even reasonable suspicion of any criminal offense?
(Paid subscribers can access video of my 2015 arrest below, along with related video from the 2014 Cato Surveillance Conference, where my question was broadcast on C-SPAN and prompted a response from an executive branch official that I found deeply unsatisfying.)
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=95459187&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6OTU0NTkxODcsImlhdCI6MTY3MzIwMzgxMSwiZXhwIjoxNjc1Nzk1ODExLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTAxODU5NyIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.7CJtQ2MvHKn9KXNi0HwAQ58Iw9c-tEwrzEPI9UnWvKI)
It was this theme of executive unaccountability that initially inspired my campaigns for Congress. I have witnessed not only 20 years of serial executive lawlessness, but also the longstanding complicity of [an oligarch in Congress whose role journalists still refuse to acknowledge](https://substack.com/redirect/b738dc92-8228-4889-92cc-3f4cd75c09b6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and recognized that the only path to institutional accountability involved removing the agencies' protectors from Congress.
Given that history, I'm thrilled to see the incoming majority [announce](https://substack.com/redirect/d44b8b09-62a8-49a2-aba8-5d7aefcc7121?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the creation of a new congressional subcommittee on the "Weaponization of the Federal Government" against the American people and our rights. This step is [long overdue](https://substack.com/redirect/8b6c0c02-3fd2-417c-ab40-abd5f063cea4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), though given the composition of Congress, I have little hope that it will achieve the same impacts as the Church Committee that exposed COINTELPRO, including crimes by the CIA and the FBI in a previous era.
### **Hope? Not so much**
While it's encouraging to see the incoming House majority take more seriously than its predecessor the responsibility to oversee the executive branch, there's no reason to think that it will do much--or even anything at all--to effectively curtail the influence of the military-industrial complex in Washington.
Ultimately, the GOP is no less committed to subsidies for weapons companies, or fossil fuel corporations, than Democrats. And as long as those industries remain the leading beneficiaries of Washington's bipartisan largesse, the future will continue to pay a price.
### **" Progressive" Democrats let GOP maniacs steal their lunch**
Given all these machinations of populist wings in both parties against their respective corporate centers, one might expect progressive Democrats to recognize the tactical success of their GOP counterparts--whether to emulate them or instead better counter them. If nothing else, they at least reflect the increasingly visible failure of corporate politics.
One would, in that case, be disappointed.
Rather than recognize the destabilization of the same corporate center that they claim to have gone to Washington to challenge, progressive Democrats have instead joined the centrist establishment, parroting its talking points against bogeymen including the populism they themselves claim to embody. Characterizing dissent within the GOP as chaotic, they have declined to learn the lesson that it could have taught them: challenging the center at critical points of accountability (ie the election of a new Speaker) can force otherwise untenable concessions.
Throughout Nancy Pelosi's tenure as House Speaker, Democrats who claimed to stand for progressive policies repeatedly caved to her political pressure, [publicly backing her](https://substack.com/redirect/e310f86b-5c55-49f7-b1f5-789dc1c01a54?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) even while she blocked Congress from debating & voting on their signature policy priorities.
In the summer of 2020, the one place in America where you could not find any debate over universal healthcare was inside the U.S. Congress. The entire progressive caucus claims to support it, while also supporting an insider trading oligarch whose opposition to their consensus has grown legendarily farcical.
Similarly, advocates for the Green New Deal--including one whose [very first act](https://substack.com/redirect/ecae0e5c-337c-45a8-8488-a6fa29294376?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in Congress involved a sit-in at Pelosi's office to champion it--have [thrown their support](https://substack.com/redirect/e310f86b-5c55-49f7-b1f5-789dc1c01a54?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) behind corrupt centrist leaders, even while those leaders kick them down the stairs in public and betray their goals in the shadows, prompting the ire of grassroots activists who might feel some measure of disappointment as a result.
While vastly overstating the case by comparison, the pattern brings to mind Kyrsten Sinema.
Briahna Joy Gray offered an excellent critique of progressive Democrats, noting their abject failure to rise to the moment.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/3af5289a-f25c-472f-898a-666bfc88ae44?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Thomas Frank, author of "Listen, Liberal" and "What's the Matter with Kansas" [joined](https://substack.com/redirect/3af5289a-f25c-472f-898a-666bfc88ae44?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) her after penning [a thoughtful op-ed](https://substack.com/redirect/37c2b1a4-bfa9-40e8-b2b7-482550a6a7f8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for the _New York Times_. Among the themes they explored was how Democrats have long created opportunities for right wing GOP populists by abandoning labor starting in the 1990s.
When Newt Gingrich became Speaker of the House in 1995, ending decades of Democratic majorities, he rode a wave of broad-based anti-elite outrage that has demonstrably inflected every election since then. Similarly, when the Tea Party won the House in 2010, it delivered President Obama what he called "a shellacking" only two years after the GOP was widely disgraced by the 2008 financial crisis. How did the GOP overcome a crisis in public confidence? It was largely because Obama "totally dropped the ball" on holding accountable the architects of the financial crisis, instead inviting several of them into his administration as it proceeded to bail out banks but never homeowners or renters.
It is the shameless allegiance between Democrats and Wall Street--which has grown only stronger in the years since then--that has opened the door for the GOP's majorities.
Yet instead of challenging the insider trading Wall Street millionaires who dominate the Democratic Party, the upstarts within it who claim the populist mantle have instead absurdly aligned with their party's elites, creating even more opportunities for right wing self-styled "populists" who remain willing to give voice--even if disingenuously--to the frustration of millions of Americans abandoned by Washington long ago.
Paid subscribers can access below a video of my arrest in the U.S. Senate in 2015, prompted by a question I was well within my rights to pose that no one else in Washington has ever demonstrated the independence to either ask or answer. Paid subscribers can also access video and a short transcript of my related comments from the 2014 Cato Surveillance Conference that were broadcast at the time on C-SPAN...

clean_body: An uprising within the GOP should shame "progressive" Democrats | | ---|---|--- | | | or ? ? An uprising within the GOP should shame "progressive" Democrats | | | Jan 8| | | ∙| | Preview ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | | ? This week, the Washington punditry critiqued a seeming exhibition of congressional dysfunction as the new GOP House majority struggled to reach consensus before eventually making Rep. Kevin McCarthy (R-CA) the new Speaker of the House. Dissent within the Republican ranks forced 15 rounds of voting before Trump's reported , coupled with a variety of to the populist flank, finally won McCarthy a majority after several days of voting. | | ---|---|--- Many of McCarthy's concessions to the GOP right wing have been criticized by both Democrats and centrist Republicans. Some of those concessions, however--not to mention the tactics that secured them--offer potentially surprising lessons for populists in the Democratic Party who have yet to achieve the same influence as those in the GOP. While Republicans may be caught in the mounting crosswinds of populist outrage towards Washington, the corporate center continues to dominate the Democratic Party. History might not repeat itself, but it certainly rhymes On the first anniversary of the January 6 insurrection, a pair of members of Congress engaged in on the House floor for the first time in decades. One, Mike Rogers (R-AL), was restrained by another, Richard Hudson (R-NC), while angrily approaching a third, Matt Gaetz (R-FL), seemingly in retaliation for Gaetz's refusal to support McCarthy in the 14th round of voting. Lost on no one was the timing of the event: exactly two years--to the very date--of the violent insurrection that shook the world's confidence in Washington and led to dozens of arrests and convictions of participants across the United States. Looming in the future are the likely repetitions of other recent examples of government dysfunction, particularly the that shuttered government agencies across the country in 1995, 2013, 2018, and 2019. With the GOP's populist majority emboldened by the concessions they won from McCarthy (including a reduced threshold for considering a new Speaker, limits on government & Pentagon spending, and separate votes on each of the government appropriation bills that Democrats have historically lumped together in order to derail debate & deliberation), they are well poised to repeat the tactics that previously forced federal agencies to furlough hundreds of thousands of employees around the country who for months at a time. Democracy is at risk, but Democrats mistake the source of the threat Many Democrats have the GOP's weak commitment to democracy, while conveniently overlooking their own party's thoroughly documented complicity in authoritarianism. The January 6 insurrection certainly offended democracy. Responding to congressional certification of a presidential vote by storming the Capitol is hardly a reflection of a peaceful transition of power. As I've noted elsewhere, the January 6 insurrection showed how little right wing populists actually care about the "law & order" they claim to champion when confronting advocates for police reform or abolition, while also demonstrating the corresponding Washington's various duplicative and fraudulent "security" budgets, as well as the community of law enforcement professionals who proved to be in the insurrection. But the insurrection offended democracy no less so than the preceding decades of congressional consensus supporting runaway increases in military spending, recurring tax cuts for millionaires, bailouts for businesses, and subsidies for fossil fuel corporations at the same time that Congress has frozen the federal minimum wage for well over a decade. Each of these decisions reflect a broad bipartisan consensus among politicians supported by corporate interests, conspicuously ignoring the voices of the millions of people across America and around the world who are impacted by them. The biggest threat to democracy in America is the supporting impunity for the executive branch and its continuing violations of civil liberties and civil rights across the United States. Whining about democracy while focusing on the theatrics of the GOP majority risks leaping from a frying pan into a fire, or perhaps ducking into a punch. The biggest threat to democracy in America is not the specter of confusion surrounding our elections. They have exhibited irregularities since at least while at best being reduced to corporate fundraising contests, all ultimately steered by the , , and self-promoting of journalists beholden to the establishment. Even if attacks on voting rights and procedural democracy were the greatest threat to our Republic, Democrats would remain complicit. They continue to fight to from ballot access, and have long played an active role in the arms race that has reduced elections to corporate fundraising contests. Some Democrats, including the current President of the United States and the former Speaker of the House, have even openly advocated for--and even gone so far as to policies after winning office as Democrats. Any of these measures verge on election fraud. Together, they reveal the hypocrisy of Democrats who claim to care about democracy. The biggest threat to democracy in America remains the supporting impunity for the executive branch and its continuing violations of civil liberties and civil rights across the United States. That theme has defined my work for well over a decade, and the emergence of support within Washington for long overdue scrutiny of the defense and intelligence establishment's corruption is welcome. It is also deeply ironic, considering the source. Can you hear me now? I've been publicly pleading for nearly 20 years for Congress to finally investigate the national security agencies that it caught in the 1970s after decades of assaulting peaceful Americans and their constitutional rights. I've of , at of around the country, and in more than I can for democratically elected policymakers to scrutinize--and impose limiting--executive branch agencies that have run amok for three generations. My arrest in the U.S. Senate in 2015 was prompted by my posing a question that no policymaker has ever demonstrated the independence to ask: Why do executive branch officials who get caught lying to Congress enjoy prestige and public pensions, while powerless Black Americans are routinely killed without charge, trial, or even reasonable suspicion of any criminal offense? (Paid subscribers can access video of my 2015 arrest below, along with related video from the 2014 Cato Surveillance Conference, where my question was broadcast on C-SPAN and prompted a response from an executive branch official that I found deeply unsatisfying.) clean_body: Let me explain | = Hey Peter, this is Rep. Mark Takano. I’m reaching out because I have a really important favor to ask you. = Let me explain: From the start, this movement was built by grassroots activists and everyday Americans who stepped up in small but important ways to help elect a gay, Japanese-American, teacher, and progressive (me!) to Congress. Since then, you all have helped me accomplish so much -- we’ve fought for LGBTQ+ rights, stood up to MAGA extremism, and championed veterans’ well-being. To this day, everything we’ve worked for has continued to rely on small-dollar donors like YOU, Peter -- not big Super PACs and billionaires like so many of my counterparts in the GOP. Peter -- let’s prove together that $1 can change the world. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = = = = = Thank you, Mark Takano | | Paid for by Mark Takano For Congress P.O. name: VA Dems Election Team
subject: Tuesday’s Elections
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Democratic Party of Virginia, www
body:
It’s time to get out the vote across the commonwealth!

|
---
Derek, It’s Get Out The Vote time in Virginia! The upcoming special elections in HD-24, HD-35, and SD-07 are really important — not just for the people who live in those districts, but for the Commonwealth as a whole. Holding onto our House seats is critical to keeping Glenn Youngkin and Todd Gilbert honest and accountable. [**Can we count on you to donate to our GOTV efforts today?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57071948/386787410/-1524691769?refcode=dpva-em-230108-GOTVspecials&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDIzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NWQ3MzU3Mi1hZjhmLWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Z3SrFqx5iVIIQ9TfDe2hoDabSEhHIxQodDFIhihE6GU=) Democrats are playing in historically Republican districts in rural Virginia and are making ground. [**Chip in to help us keep laying the groundwork and turn out voters on Tuesday and beyond!**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57071949/386787411/-1524691769?refcode=dpva-em-230108-GOTVspecials&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDIzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NWQ3MzU3Mi1hZjhmLWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Z3SrFqx5iVIIQ9TfDe2hoDabSEhHIxQodDFIhihE6GU=) **The race in SD-07 may be the most critical of them all — the future of reproductive freedom in Virginia could be decided by the winner of this matchup.** With a slim Democratic margin in the Virginia Senate, this is the last wall in place to prevent Youngkin’s dream of an abortion ban across the Commonwealth. **We need to add another brick to our “Blue Wall” in the Senate —[can we count on your support to defend our abortion rights and Get Out The Vote in SD-07?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57071950/386787412/-1524691769?refcode=dpva-em-230108-GOTVspecials&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDIzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NWQ3MzU3Mi1hZjhmLWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Z3SrFqx5iVIIQ9TfDe2hoDabSEhHIxQodDFIhihE6GU=)** There is so much at stake on Tuesday. We hope you’ll help us make the difference in these elections, and protect our Commonwealth in the process. clean_body: It’s time to get out the vote across the commonwealth! | Derek, It’s Get Out The Vote time in Virginia! The upcoming special elections in HD-24, HD-35, and SD-07 are really important — not just for the people who live in those districts, but for the Commonwealth as a whole. Holding onto our House seats is critical to keeping Glenn Youngkin and Todd Gilbert honest and accountable. = Democrats are playing in historically Republican districts in rural Virginia and are making ground. = The race in SD-07 may be the most critical of them all — the future of reproductive freedom in Virginia could be decided by the winner of this matchup. With a slim Democratic margin in the Virginia Senate, this is the last wall in place to prevent Youngkin’s dream of an abortion ban across the Commonwealth. We need to add another brick to our “Blue Wall” in the Senate —= There is so much at stake on Tuesday. We hope you’ll help us make the difference in these elections, and protect our Commonwealth in the process. VA Dems Election Team | =****** =******** [content goes here] Democratic Party of Virginia PO Box 448 Richmond, VA 23218 United States Paid for by the Democratic Party of Virginia, www.vademocrats.org. subject: Reserve your DVD box set
body:
C.S. Lewis was a master at addressing life’s most important questions through vivid language

| This sponsored message from Conservatives Give is on behalf of Hillsdale College | | [](https://links.conservativesgive.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=666c42bbd41c4b3b82133ae971878b55&_e=fDQaw0S0uakzLpeO1Wa5RXQIYZmdRpJcmp1XKA3nKMg8hHIf2XQarQLmLArG4BoNp1hWzDIyVItUfLvVeRKSnM98aBXAk3AkehpgISboR6YUquDD2-m_lc5UVG5NuPb10ilchdQZKqg6KiSNFPefadqk4KXHvuKOMD_yF9cRS9Hv3dyWLmXUE5LHdl59fTxMTF9JPi69dZ4XHNGO7nBuEmD9IKCCzIyhAs5JJUxgoBq6fykiwp3G4RWWvzwFcO_Afx2vh1qCi8Y5Ibs4Z9iDwIvHgQ4169lYlLvZWYf0O5NpNz9u2DWDX8Vut-j_43D8Hf1dYZ9V3moGqSevf3PS15KSVtpZiE4e1KN47-P7-tZcVbXn6m5bofrY-GgxZT0WLXghJ1mCSdIWkmfvQhIAGIklpa-wWz9-rj6y8KtFVBC5Y3akujEN-wTwDAH-y-7x)
---
| | Dear friend, C.S. Lewis was a master at addressing life's most important questions through vivid language that is delightful and captivating to read.

His signature ability to communicate the truth about Christianity has inspired countless skeptics to become believers and even more believers to renew and deepen their faith--making him the greatest Christian apologist of the twentieth century.

That's why we produced "[**C.S. Lewis on Christianity,**](https://links.conservativesgive.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=666c42bbd41c4b3b82133ae971878b55&_e=fDQaw0S0uakzLpeO1Wa5RXQIYZmdRpJcmp1XKA3nKMg8hHIf2XQarQLmLArG4BoNp1hWzDIyVItUfLvVeRKSnM98aBXAk3AkehpgISboR6YUquDD2-m_lc5UVG5NuPb10ilchdQZKqg6KiSNFPefadqk4KXHvuKOMD_yF9cRS9Hv3dyWLmXUE5LHdl59fTxMTF9JPi69dZ4XHNGO7nBuEmD9IKCCzIyhAs5JJUxgoBq6fykiwp3G4RWWvzwFcO_Afx2vh1qCi8Y5Ibs4Z9iDwC9GsaFOQ4VYW96kGoUvtjEz1Z7PvWhrhn1mTvc1r6krpsk3RV2ay6bah0bEmdZZV_TwPyuXfZj0P6ivxDHhNgQQtIBwts4Pl36H8jU48wkUKZHqaAwXpwCzGTHcz84cPKX1vuKGWPyyAg0cTY6k4g_WRJK76B5n9OExTYxGdwXj)" which you can now own on DVD.
---
| [](https://links.conservativesgive.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=666c42bbd41c4b3b82133ae971878b55&_e=fDQaw0S0uakzLpeO1Wa5RXQIYZmdRpJcmp1XKA3nKMg8hHIf2XQarQLmLArG4BoNp1hWzDIyVItUfLvVeRKSnM98aBXAk3AkehpgISboR6YUquDD2-m_lc5UVG5NuPb10ilchdQZKqg6KiSNFPefadqk4KXHvuKOMD_yF9cRS9Hv3dyWLmXUE5LHdl59fTxMTF9JPi69dZ4XHNGO7nBuEmD9IKCCzIyhAs5JJUxgoBq6fykiwp3G4RWWvzwFcO_Afx2vh1qCi8Y5Ibs4Z9iDwGEAoNOGw4xXpUfOfnbitkJxt7ur6_oWWBkVnwK-5JLseOruBgqGaUYwDVXRIyiqAqCMmx_Rgp1B_8luazrCQisVpLOyf2oFe0Vh5wuHaAP73GfPjH1oDInyxGjwwkKypv5FIaiGAvrgP2S091R0twsTqFjfq8xJYdhmyThvajzQ)
---
| | In this course you will learn Lewis's profound insights on the central topics of Christianity, including:
* good and evil,
* conversion and faith,
* prayer and the Bible,
* suffering and death,
* heaven and hell, and much more.

Dr. Ward is a Distinguished Visiting Professor at Hillsdale College and a member of the Faculty of Theology and Religion at the University of Oxford. He is the author of _Planet Narnia: The Seven Heavens in the Imagination of C.S. Lewis_ and _After Humanity: A Guide to C.S. Lewis 's The Abolition of Man_, and the co-editor of _The Cambridge Companion to C.S. Lewis_. He even spent three years living in Lewis's Oxford home as the resident Warden of The Kilns!

This course is especially moving because it examines C.S. Lewis's beautiful writings about faith and traces Lewis's own journey from skeptic to believer. The combination provides an opportunity for us to grow in our knowledge and understanding of Christianity.

That's why I hope you'll join us in examining the eternal questions of Christianity with C.S. Lewis with [this beautiful new DVD box set](https://links.conservativesgive.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=666c42bbd41c4b3b82133ae971878b55&_e=fDQaw0S0uakzLpeO1Wa5RXQIYZmdRpJcmp1XKA3nKMg8hHIf2XQarQLmLArG4BoNp1hWzDIyVItUfLvVeRKSnM98aBXAk3AkehpgISboR6YUquDD2-m_lc5UVG5NuPb10ilchdQZKqg6KiSNFPefadqk4KXHvuKOMD_yF9cRS9Hv3dyWLmXUE5LHdl59fTxMTF9JPi69dZ4XHNGO7nBuEmD9IKCCzIyhAs5JJUxgoBq6fykiwp3G4RWWvzwFcO_Afx2vh1qCi8Y5Ibs4Z9iDwHUxIs2ybbToD-zN-6e6DNInFLO1A5Ptqeiq3h8_yd1OoC5x7twANtiukXDHB1pNWmpGiA1BXTSbHQpMyDWU8d5HRCFUqTJ6h4QRNaXEpY_PW9R44OspOlpqv_DuPZH55pZdD8dQIVEYIlDCATOSTiYX-9z4adYfji4qZTRiMmfL).

Best regards,

Kyle Murnen, '09
Director of Online Learning
Hillsdale College
